name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-7
title: .rabbit/ not on scope-guard allowlist — rabbit-feature-touch protocol requires session override for impl-suggestion and tdd-report writes
description:
The scope-guard filename allowlist (scope-guard.py:96) permits writes to settings.json, settings.local.json, .gitignore, and .rabbit-scope-override unconditionally. It does NOT cover .rabbit/ at the repo root.

The rabbit-feature-touch protocol requires writes to .rabbit/ as part of its normal execution:
- rabbit-spec (Step 3) writes .rabbit/impl-suggestion-<feature>.json
- TDD subagent (Step 8) writes .rabbit/tdd-report-<feature>.json

Neither the per-feature scope marker (.rabbit-scope-active-<feature>) nor any other scope mechanism covers .rabbit/ — per-feature markers only permit writes inside the feature's own directory under .claude/features/. The only way to write to .rabbit/ without a scope marker is via the session override, which is semantically wrong: override is for human-approved exceptional bypasses, not for routine workflow writes.

Observed during fix of RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-6 (2026-05-17): required echo session > .rabbit-scope-override before mkdir -p .rabbit and Write to .rabbit/impl-suggestion-rabbit-cage.json. The TDD subagent also needed the override active to write its tdd-report.

Root cause: .rabbit/ is a known gitignored runtime artifact directory owned by the rabbit workflow, analogous to the already-allowlisted .claude/bugs/ and .claude/backlogs/ paths (scope-guard.py:99-103). It should be on the same unconditional allowlist so rabbit-feature-touch dispatchers can write impl-suggestion and tdd-report files using only the per-feature scope marker.

Fix: add .rabbit/ prefix check to scope-guard.py alongside the existing .claude/bugs/ and .claude/backlogs/ checks. Update the spec (Invariant 20) to list .rabbit/ as an always-permitted write target.

history.note: Fixed by PR #98: added .rabbit/ to scope-guard.py path-prefix allowlist. rabbit-feature-touch dispatchers no longer need session override for impl-suggestion/tdd-report writes.
